{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"**Merge Decision: Block**

This PR should not merge as-is. The blocker is not every finding; it is the concentration of high/medium agent-risk in the core CLI implementation plus weak tests around error/exit behavior. The scan found 60 findings: 3 high, 40 medium, 17 low.

**Block Merge**

| Finding | Decision | Why |
|---|---:|---|
| `crime_00001` `large_file` `lib/command.js` | Block | High severity, `agent_risk: 0.70`. A 2,179-line core command module with 150 top-level functions is the main operational surface of the CLI. This is too much hidden coupling for an initial merge. |
| `crime_00002` `large_file` `typings/index.d.ts` | Block | High severity, `agent_risk: 0.70`. Public typings are product surface. An 891-line declaration file with 121 top-level functions is hard to review and easy to drift from implementation. |
| `crime_00003` `large_function` `_executeSubCommand` | Block | High severity, `agent_risk: 0.69`. Subcommand execution is high-blast CLI behavior. A 120-line method is likely mixing resolution, process spawning, error handling, and control flow. |
| `crime_00020` `large_function` `parseOptions` | Block | Medium severity, `agent_risk: 0.61`. Option parsing is central Commander behavior. At 115 lines with `test_gap: 1`, future edits are likely to miss edge cases. |
| `crime_00021`-`crime_00028` weak tests in `tests/command.exitOverride.test.js` | Block | Medium severity, each `agent_risk: 0.46`. These cover exit override, help/version exits, mandatory options, choices, and executable subcommands, but contain zero assertions. That gives false confidence around critical CLI failure paths. |
| `crime_00029`-`crime_00036` weak tests in `tests/ts-imports.test.ts` | Block | Medium severity, each `agent_risk: 0.46`. Import/type compatibility tests with no assertions may be acceptable only if they are compile-only tests. As written in runtime test files, they should assert exported shapes or be moved to a type-test mechanism. |

**Warn**

| Finding | Decision | Why |
|---|---:|---|
| `crime_00004` example duplicate `myParseInt` | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.62`. Example duplication is not a merge blocker, but shared docs/examples can diverge. |
| `crime_00005`-`crime_00008` duplicates between `lib/argument.js` and `lib/option.js` | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.62`. These are production duplicates in related option/argument abstractions. Not necessarily fatal, but should be refactored or intentionally documented before much more work lands. |
| `crime_00009`-`crime_00019` duplicate test blocks | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.47`. Test helper duplication creates maintenance drag, but lower risk than production duplication because `test_gap: 0`. |
| `crime_00037` large anonymous test function | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.45`. Large test body is maintainability risk, not a merge blocker by itself. |
| `crime_00038` magic literal `"PORT"` scattered across examples/typings | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.60`. Could be legitimate CLI example data, but repeated environment-variable/domain literals often become accidental policy. |
| `crime_00039` `_parseCommand` large function | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.60`. Core parser path, close to blocker territory; should be split soon. |
| `crime_00040` `addOption` large function | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.59`. Slightly over threshold but in central command configuration code. |
| `crime_00041` `typings/index.test-d.ts` large file | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.59`. Type tests are allowed to be broad, but 480 lines should probably be split by API area. |
| `crime_00042` `lib/help.js` large file | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.59`. Help formatting is user-visible and easy to regress; split when touched. |
| `crime_00043` `lib/option.js` large file | Warn | Medium, `agent_risk: 0.57`. Only slightly over threshold, but part of core CLI semantics. |
| `crime_00044`-`crime_00045` broken zh-CN doc links | Warn | Low severity, `agent_risk: 0.51`. Not merge-blocking for runtime, but stale docs can mislead agents and users. |
| `crime_00047`-`crime_00050` large test `describe` callbacks | Warn | Low severity, `agent_risk: 0.34-0.35`. Split opportunistically; not worth blocking initial merge alone. |
| `crime_00051`-`crime_00059` weak assertion matchers | Warn | Low severity, `agent_risk: 0.34`. These tests have some assertions, but weak ones. Improve when touching the related behavior. |

**Ignore**

| Finding | Decision | Why |
|---|---:|---|
| `crime_00046` commented-out code in executable subcommand search test | Ignore | Low severity, `agent_risk: 0.36`. Small two-line comment in a test. Worth cleanup, but not material to merge. |
| `crime_00060` logic in comment about `SIGUSR1` | Ignore | Low severity, `agent_risk: 0.31`, confidence only `0.65`. It may just be historical test rationale. Convert to a test if this signal handling is still relevant. |

**Required Before Merge**

Reduce the high-risk core surface: split or isolate `lib/command.js`, especially `_executeSubCommand`, `parseOptions`, and `_parseCommand`; split or justify the public typings structure; and fix the zero-assertion tests around `exitOverride` and TypeScript imports. The remaining duplicate/test-size/doc findings can be tracked as follow-up warnings."}}
